gates.title: Architecture and migration foundation
criteria: The architecture constitution is internally consistent.
Every design-book section has one truthful evidence and gap entry.

gates.title: Canonical schemas and generated contracts
criteria: Shared facts have strict canonical schemas.
Generated bindings and documents are reproducible in one pass.

gates.title: Virtual receiver and deterministic replay
criteria: The simulator exercises generation, presence, qualification, transport, and restoration without hardware authority.
Committed replay is sanitized and deterministic.

gates.title: Bridge, ownership, restoration, and SDK
criteria: The bridge is the sole userspace receiver writer.
SDK requests are versioned, bounded, lease-owned, generation-bound, and conservatively reconciled.

gates.title: Kernel transport and activation
criteria: The module builds warning-clean against supported headers.
Input preservation, generations, passive observation, and bounded writer transport are qualified on an exact candidate.
remaining: Execute targeted installed-candidate input-preservation, reconnect, suspend, and writer-session qualification on supported hardware.

gates.title: Application integrations
criteria: Adapters use only the SDK and preserve unrelated devices.
OpenRGB, Polychromatic, and optional compatibility behavior are qualified as installed candidates.
remaining: Qualify the rewritten OpenRGB integration and selected application coexistence paths against the exact release candidate.

gates.title: Native package lifecycle
criteria: Arch, Debian, and RPM artifacts derive from one canonical root and are reproducible.
Fresh install, upgrade, rollback, uninstall, activation, and configuration preservation pass in disposable native systems.
remaining: Run complete package-manager lifecycle scenarios in disposable Arch, Debian, and RPM-family images.

gates.title: Complete software verification
criteria: Every full-software graph node passes from a clean source revision.
No software result is presented as physical qualification or publication authorization.

gates.title: Targeted hardware qualification
criteria: Every promoted writable capability has exact candidate-bound physical evidence.
Unknown models remain safe and non-writable.
remaining: Select the exact release candidate and execute the bounded qualification plan without importing laboratory writers into the product.

gates.title: Explicit publication decision
criteria: Every preceding release-blocking gate is satisfied.
A human publication decision explicitly authorizes the chosen channel and artifacts.
remaining: Product publication remains unauthorized. Do not create a release, tag, package channel, or supported-product claim from software evidence alone.
